metadata.summary: Nillion Nada DSL to create Nillion MPC programs.

documentation:
# nada-dsl

nada-dsl is a Python DSL for building MPC programs on the Nillion Network.

See the official Nillion documentation site for more about the [Nada Language][nada-language] and
[Nada By Example][examples].

[examples]: https://docs.nillion.com/nada-by-example
[nada-language]: https://docs.nillion.com/nada-lang

documentation: Default implementation for the Conversion of a type into MIR representation.

methods.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.NadaType.class_to_mir
documentation: Converts a class into a MIR Nada type.

documentation: Returns True if the type is a scalar.

methods.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.NadaType.is_literal
documentation: Returns True if the type is a literal.

Nada type class.

This is the parent class of all nada types.

In Nada, all the types wrap Operations. For instance, an addition between two integers
is represented like this SecretInteger(child=Addition(...)).

In MIR, the representation is based around operations. A MIR operation points to other
operations and has a return type.

To aid MIR conversion, we store in memory (`AST_OPERATIONS`) all the operations in a
MIR-friendly format, as subclasses of ASTOperation.

Whenever the Python interpreter constructs an instance of NadaType, it will also store
in memory the corresponding child operation. In order to do so, the ASTOperation will
need the type in MIR format. Which is why all instances of `NadaType` provide an implementation
of `to_mir()`.

modules.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.function
documentation:
function.py

Nada function definitions and utilities.

functions.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.function.contained_types
documentation: Utility function that calculates the child type for a function argument.

functions.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.function.nada_fn
documentation:
Can be used also for lambdas
```python
array.map(
    nada_fn(
        lambda x: x.cast(SecretInteger),
        args_ty={'x': SecretInteger}, return_ty=SecretInteger))
```

documentation: Store this Nada Function in AST.

Nada Function.

Represents a Nada Function. Nada functions are special types of functions that are used
in map / reduce operations.

They are decorated using the `@nada_fn` decorator.

methods.name: nada_dsl.ast_util.ASTOperation.to_mir
documentation: Converts this AST Operation into a valid MIR data structure

AST Operations.

Base abstract class for all AST Operations. Defines the minimum contract
that needs to be implemented by any subclass.

Attributes
----------
id: int
    The operation identifier. This is the unique identifier assigned by Python
    that is the return value of id().

source_ref: SourceRef
    The source reference object

ty: str | Dict[str, Dict]
    The type representation, it can be a string (e.g. "SecretInteger") or a dictionary
    for compount types.

classes.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.scalar_types.ScalarType

methods.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.scalar_types.ScalarType.to_public
documentation: Convert this scalar type into a public variable.

The Nada Scalar type.
This is the super class for all scalar types in Nada.
These are:
    - Boolean, PublicBoolean, SecretBoolean
    - Integer, PublicInteger, SecretInteger
    - UnsignedInteger, PublicUnsignedInteger, SecretUnsignedInteger
`ScalarType` provides common operation implementations for all the scalar types
based on the typing rules of the Nada model.

classes.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.scalar_types.NumericType

methods.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.scalar_types.NumericType.__radd__
documentation: This adds support for builtin `sum` operation for numeric types.

The superclass of all the numeric types in Nada:
- Integer, PublicInteger, SecretInteger
- UnsignedInteger, PublicUnsignedInteger, SecretUnsignedInteger
It provides common operation implementations for all the numeric types based
on the typing rules of the Nada model.

classes.name: nada_dsl.nada_types.scalar_types.BooleanType

modules.name: nada_dsl.audit.common
documentation:
Common classes and functions for the Nada DSL auditing component
(used across different static analysis submodules).

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.typeerror_demote
documentation: Demote a direct-cause type error to a generic (possibly indirect) type error.

documentation: Set, update, or delete an {obj}`ast` node's static analysis attribute.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.rules_no_restriction

Delete the rule attributes of an {obj}`ast` node (and possibly those of its
descendants).

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.unify
documentation: Perform basic type unification of Python types.

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.Rule
documentation: Base class for violations of rules defined by static analysis submodules.

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.RuleInAncestor
documentation:
Rule attribute value that indicates that the Nada DSL rule attribute of an
{obj}`ast` node is determined by an ancestor node's Nada DSL rule attribute.

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.SyntaxRestriction
documentation:
Base class for violations of syntax restrictions defined by static analysis
submodules.

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.TypeErrorRoot
documentation: Class for type errors that are not caused by other type errors.

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.TypeInParent
documentation:
Type attribute value that indicates that the Nada DSL type attribute of an
{obj}`ast` node is determined by the parent node's Nada DSL type attribute.

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.common.Feedback
documentation:
Feedback aggregator (used throughout the recursive static analysis
algorithms in order to collect all created exceptions).

modules.name: nada_dsl.audit

Parse a Python source string that represents a Nada DSL program

modules.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend
documentation:
Compiler frontend consisting of wrapper functions for the classes and functions
that constitute the Nada embedded domain-specific language (EDSL).

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.get_target_dir
documentation: Get the target directory for compilation output.

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.nada_compile
documentation: Compile Nada to MIR and dump it as JSON.

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.nada_dsl_to_nada_mir
documentation: Convert Nada DSL to Nada MIR.

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.to_party_list
documentation: Convert parties to a list in MIR format.

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.to_input_list
documentation: Convert inputs to a list in MIR format.

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.to_literal_list
documentation: Convert literals to a list in MIR format.

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.to_mir_function_list
documentation:
Convert functions to a list in MIR format.

From a starting dictionary of functions, it traverses each one of them,
generating the corresponding MIR representation, discovering all the operations
in the function.

The algorithm might find new function calls while processing the operations
in a function. These function calls might discover of new functions that are not
in the original dictionary. These functions will be processed in turn.

This function is designed to be invoked after the initial operation discovery
which will find the starting set of functions.

Arguments
---------
functions: Dict[int, NadaFunctionASTOperation]
    A dictionary containing a starting list of functions

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.add_input_to_map
documentation: Adds an input to the global INPUTS dictionary

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.traverse_and_process_operations
documentation:
Traverses the AST operations finding all the operation tree rooted at the given
operation. Uses an iterative DFS algorithm.

It invokes `process_operation` which in turn generates a MIR and optionally discover
extra functions.

Arguments
---------
operation_id: int
    The identifier of the root operation where the algorithm will start traversing the
    operation graph
operations: Dict[int, Dict]
    Dictionary that will be updated with the operations found
functions: Dict[int, NadaFunctionASTOperation]
    Dictionary of existing functions. If a function is found that is not in this dictionary
    it will added to the result dictionary

Returns
-------
Dict[int, NadaFunctionASTOperation]
    Dictionary with all the new functions being found while traversing the operation tree

functions.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.process_operation
documentation:
Process an AST operation.

For arithmetic operations it simply returns the MIR representation of the operation.

For inputs and literal types, it adds the corresponding value to the appropriate
dictionaries and returns the MIR representation.

For map, reduce and function call operations, adds the nada function if it's not in the
functions dictionary, and returns the MIR representation.

Whenever it finds a nada function, it adds it if it's not there. But it does not generate
a MIR representation as functions are processed separately.

It ignores nada function arguments as they should not be present in the MIR.

documentation: Custom JSON encoder for classes.

classes.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.CompilerException
documentation: Generic compiler exception

classes.name: nada_dsl.compiler_frontend.ProcessOperationOutput
documentation: Output of the process_operation function

modules.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict
documentation:
Static analysis submodule that defines the highly limited "strict"
subset of the Nada DSL syntax.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict._rules_restrictions_descendants
documentation:
Remove all rule attributes that are redundant because they occur in a
an ancestor.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict.rules
documentation:
Mark all {obj}`ast` nodes as prohibited (as a starting point for a strict
static analysis).

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict._types_base
documentation:
Return a boolean value indicating whether the supplied type is a base
type.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict._types_list_monomorphic
documentation:
Return a boolean value indicating whether the supplied type represents a
monomorphic list type (*i.e.*, a list type wherein the types of the items
are fully specified).

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict._types_list_monomorphic_depth
documentation: Return an integer representing the depth of the monomorphic list type.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict._types_monomorphic
documentation:
Return a boolean value indicating whether the supplied type represents a
monomorphic type permitted by this static analysis submodule.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict._types_binop_mult_add_sub
documentation:
Determine the result type for multiplication and addition operations
involving Nada DSL integers.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict._types_compare
documentation:
Determine the result type for comparison operations involving Nada DSL
integers.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict.types
documentation:
Infer types of {obj}`ast` where possible, adding the type (or error)
information in node attributes (and removing syntax restriction attributes
as appropriate).

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.strict.strict
documentation:
Take a Python source file representing a Nada DSL program, statically
analyze it (against the "strict" Nada DSL subset), and generate an
interactive HTML report detailing the results.

modules.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract
documentation:
Abstract interpreter and type definitions for the Nada DSL auditing
component.

functions.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.signature
documentation:
Return the signature of the supplied Nada program (represented as a
string). The signature consists of three lists: the program's (1) parties,
(2) inputs, and (3) outputs.

```pycon
>>> source = '\n'.join([
...     'from nada_dsl import *',
...     '',
...     'def nada_main():',
...     '    party1 = Party(name="Party1")',
...     '    a = SecretInteger(Input(name="a", party=party1))',
...     '    b = SecretInteger(Input(name="b", party=party1))',
...     '',
...     '    c = a * b',
...     '',
...     '    return [Output(c, "c", party1), Output(c, "d", party1)]'
... ])
>>> (ps, ins, outs) = signature(source)
>>> [p.name for p in ps]
['Party1']
>>> [i.name for i in ins]
['a', 'b']
>>> [o.name for o in outs]
['c', 'd']
```

If the supplied strign is not a valid Nada program, an exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> source = '\n'.join([
...     'from nada_dsl import *',
...     'def nada_main():',
...     '    pass'
... ])
>>> signature(source)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
ValueError: nada_main must return a sequence of outputs
>>> source = '\n'.join([
...     'def nada_main():',
...     '    pass'
... ])
>>> signature(source)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
ValueError: first statement must be: from nada_dsl import *
>>> source = '\n'.join([
...     'from nada_dsl import *',
...     'def foo():',
...     '    pass'
... ])
>>> signature(source)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
ValueError: nada_main must be defined
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Metaclass

methods.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Metaclass.shape
documentation:
Return the shape of a class that is an instance of this
metaclass.

```pycon
>>> PublicInteger.shape().__name__
'Public'
>>> AbstractInteger.shape(Constant).__name__
'Integer'
```

Metaclass for the {obj}`Constant`, {obj}`Public`, and {obj}`Secret`
classes, enabling comparison of derived classes and conversions
between them.

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Constant
documentation:
Class from which classes for constants are derived.

```pycon
>>> Constant <= Constant
True
>>> Constant < Secret
True
>>> Constant < Secret
True
>>> Secret <= Constant
False
>>> min(Constant, Public).__name__
'Constant'
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Public
documentation:
Class from which classes for public values are derived.

```pycon
>>> Public < Secret
True
>>> Public <= Constant
False
>>> Public < Public
False
>>> min(Secret, Public).__name__
'Public'
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Secret
documentation:
Class from which classes for constants are derived.

```pycon
>>> Constant < Secret
True
>>> Public < Secret
True
>>> Secret < Secret
False
>>> max([Secret, Public, Constant]).__name__
'Secret'
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Abstract
documentation:
Base class for abstract interpreter values. All more specific abstract
value types are derived from this class.

The attributes of this class are also used as global aggregators of the
signature components (parties, inputs, and outputs) during abstract execution.

```pycon
>>> Abstract.initialize()
>>> party = Party("party")
>>> input = Input("input", party)
>>> output = Output(PublicInteger(input), "output", party)
>>> [
...     list(map(lambda entry: type(entry).__name__, component))
...     for component in Abstract.signature()
... ]
[['Party'], ['Input'], ['Output']]
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Party
documentation:
Abstract interpreter values corresponding to parties.

```pycon
>>> isinstance(Party("party"), Party)
True
```

If arguments having incorrect types are supplied to the constructor, an
exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> Party(123)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: name parameter must be a string
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Input

methods.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Input._value
documentation: Retrieve the value for this input when performing concrete interpretation.

Abstract interpreter values corresponding to inputs.

```pycon
>>> isinstance(Input("input", Party("party")), Input)
True
```

If arguments having incorrect types are supplied to the constructor, an
exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> Input(123, Party("party"))
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: name parameter must be a string
>>> Input("input", 456)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: party parameter must be a Party object
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Output
documentation:
Abstract interpreter values corresponding to outputs.

```pycon
>>> party = Party("party")
>>> input = Input("input", party)
>>> isinstance(Output(PublicInteger(input), "output", party), Output)
True
```

If arguments having incorrect types are supplied to the constructor, an
exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> Output(123, "output", party)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: output value must be a PublicInteger or a SecretInteger
>>> Output(PublicInteger(input), 123, party)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: name parameter must be a string
>>> Output(PublicInteger(input), "output", 123)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: party parameter must be a Party object
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.AbstractInteger

methods.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.AbstractInteger.__add__
documentation:
Addition of abstract values that are instances of integer classes.
The table below presents the output type for each combination
of argument types.

```






self

other

result

Integer

Integer

Integer

Integer

PublicInteger

PublicInteger

Integer

SecretInteger

SecretInteger

PublicInteger

Integer

PublicInteger

PublicInteger

PublicInteger

PublicInteger

PublicInteger

SecretInteger

SecretInteger

SecretInteger

Integer

SecretInteger

SecretInteger

PublicInteger

SecretInteger

SecretInteger

SecretInteger

SecretInteger
```

```pycon
>>> x = Input("x", Party("a"))
>>> y = Input("y", Party("b"))
>>> type(PublicInteger(x) + PublicInteger(y)).__name__
'PublicInteger'
>>> type(PublicInteger(x) + SecretInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(SecretInteger(x) + PublicInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(SecretInteger(x) + SecretInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
```

The addition operator supports `0` as a base case in order to
accommodate the built-in {obj}`sum` function.

```pycon
>>> type(sum([PublicInteger(x), SecretInteger(y)])).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(sum([SecretInteger(x), SecretInteger(y)])).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(sum([PublicInteger(x), PublicInteger(y)])).__name__
'PublicInteger'
```

If a value of an incompatible type is supplied to an overloaded
operator, an exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> PublicInteger(x) + 123
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: expecting Integer, PublicInteger, or SecretInteger
```

Comparison of abstract values that are instances of integer
classes. See {obj}`AbstractInteger.__lt__` for details and examples.

methods.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.AbstractInteger.__ne__
documentation:
Comparison of abstract values that are instances of integer
classes. See {obj}`AbstractInteger.__lt__` for details and examples.

Abstract interpreter values corresponding to all integers types.
This class is only used by derived classes and is not exported.

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.Integer
documentation:
Abstract values corresponding to constant integers.

```pycon
>>> Integer < PublicInteger
True
>>> Integer < SecretInteger
True
>>> Integer.shape().__name__
'Constant'
```

Instances of this class support the use of some built-in operators.

```pycon
>>> x = Input("x", Party("a"))
>>> y = Input("y", Party("b"))
>>> type(Integer(x) + Integer(y)).__name__
'Integer'
>>> type(Integer(x) * Integer(y)).__name__
'Integer'
>>> type(Integer(y) + PublicInteger(x)).__name__
'PublicInteger'
>>> type(SecretInteger(x) + Integer(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(PublicInteger(x) * Integer(y)).__name__
'PublicInteger'
>>> type(Integer(y) * SecretInteger(x)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
```

The addition operator supports `0` as a base case in order to
accommodate the built-in {obj}`sum` function.

```pycon
>>> type(sum([Integer(x), Integer(y)])).__name__
'Integer'
>>> type(sum([Integer(x), SecretInteger(y)])).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(sum([PublicInteger(x), Integer(y)])).__name__
'PublicInteger'
```

Concrete interpretation (with explicit values) is also supported if
those values are present in the aggregate context being maintained
using the static class attributes.

```pycon
>>> Abstract.context = {'x': 123, 'y': 456}
>>> r = Integer(x, 123) + SecretInteger(y, 456)
>>> r.value
579
>>> r = PublicInteger(x, 123) + Integer(y, 456)
>>> r.value
579
>>> r = SecretInteger(x, 123) * Integer(y, 456)
>>> r.value
56088
>>> r = Integer(x, 123) * PublicInteger(y, 456)
>>> r.value
56088
```

If a value of an incompatible type is supplied to an overloaded
operator, an exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> Integer(x) + 123
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: expecting Integer, PublicInteger, or SecretInteger
>>> Integer(x) * 123
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: expecting Integer, PublicInteger, or SecretInteger
>>> 123 * Integer(x)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: expecting Integer, PublicInteger, or SecretInteger
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.PublicInteger
documentation:
Abstract values corresponding to public integers.

```pycon

True
```

Instances of this class support the use of some built-in operators.

```pycon
>>> type(PublicInteger(x) * SecretInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(SecretInteger(x) * PublicInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
```

The addition operator supports `0` as a base case in order to
accommodate the built-in {obj}`sum` function.

```pycon
>>> type(sum([PublicInteger(x), SecretInteger(y)])).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(sum([PublicInteger(x), PublicInteger(y)])).__name__
'PublicInteger'
```

Concrete interpretation (with explicit values) is also supported if
those values are present in the aggregate context being maintained
using the static class attributes.

```pycon
>>> Abstract.context = {'x': 123, 'y': 456}
>>> r = PublicInteger(x, 123) + SecretInteger(y, 456)
>>> r.value
579
>>> r = PublicInteger(x, 123) + PublicInteger(y, 456)
>>> r.value
579
>>> r = PublicInteger(x, 123) * SecretInteger(y, 456)
>>> r.value
56088
>>> r = PublicInteger(x, 123) * PublicInteger(y, 456)
>>> r.value
56088
```

If a value of an incompatible type is supplied to an overloaded
operator, an exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> PublicInteger(x) + 123
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: expecting Integer, PublicInteger, or SecretInteger
>>> PublicInteger(x) * 123
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: expecting Integer, PublicInteger, or SecretInteger
>>> 123 * PublicInteger(x)
Traceback (most recent call last):

TypeError: expecting Integer, PublicInteger, or SecretInteger
```

classes.name: nada_dsl.audit.abstract.SecretInteger
documentation:
Abstract interpreter values corresponding to secret integers.

```pycon
>>> SecretInteger < Integer
False
>>> PublicInteger < SecretInteger
True
>>> SecretInteger.shape().__name__
'Secret'
```

Instances of this class support the use of some built-in operators.

```pycon
>>> x = Input("x", Party("a"))
>>> y = Input("y", Party("b"))
>>> type(Integer(x) + SecretInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(SecretInteger(x) + PublicInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(SecretInteger(x) + SecretInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(PublicInteger(x) * SecretInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(SecretInteger(x) * Integer(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
>>> type(SecretInteger(x) * SecretInteger(y)).__name__
'SecretInteger'
```

Concrete interpretation (with explicit values) is also supported if
those values are present in the aggregate context being maintained
using the static class attributes.

```pycon
>>> Abstract.context = {'x': 123, 'y': 456}
>>> r = SecretInteger(x, 123) * SecretInteger(y, 456)
>>> r.value
56088
```

If a value of an incompatible type is supplied to an overloaded
operator, an exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> SecretInteger(x) + 123
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: expecting Integer, PublicInteger, or SecretInteger
```

```pycon
>>> y = Input("y", Party("b"))
>>> z = Input("z", Party("c"))
>>> type(PublicBoolean(x).if_else(PublicInteger(y), PublicInteger(z))).__name__
```
